frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly, I never understand women who do so much makeup for everyday things. Natural look is always better yaar, but at the same time completely careless bhi nahi lagna chahiye — thoda presentable rehna should be basic. Jab itna effort visible ho, it feels like you're trying too hard for attention.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it applies a double bind to women’s appearance: they should look attractive and 'presentable' but not appear to have made visible effort. It encodes the makeup paradox, policing grooming while assuming makeup is done for attention.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
